zh.lifeSummary: Carrie Skyler Fisher是Henry Fisher和Susannah Wynne的女兒，於1505年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾巴金哈姆。她於1522年與Sir Thomas Ramsey在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1576年在英國巴金哈姆爾享年71歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Carrie Skyler Fisher是Henry Fisher和Susannah Wynne的女儿，于1505年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾巴金哈姆。她于1522年与Sir Thomas Ramsey在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1576年在英国巴金哈姆爾享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: Fisher
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a fisherman, from Middle English fis(sc)her(e) ‘fisherman’ (Old English fiscere). In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from many other languages, including German Fischer and its Slavic(ized) variant Fišer (see Fiser ), Dutch Visser , Hungarian Halász (see Halasz ), Italian Pescatore , Slovenian Ribič (see Ribic ), and Croatian Ribić or Ribar .

English: in a few cases, possibly a topographic name for someone who lived near a fish weir on a river, from Middle English fis(sc)hwere, fisshyar ‘fish weir’ (Old English fiscwer, fiscgear), or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Fisher in North Mundham, Sussex.
Irish: translation into English of Gaelic Ó Bradáin ‘descendant of Bradán’, a personal name meaning ‘salmon’. See Braden .

variants.name: Fischer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Erwin, Gerhard, Helmut, Fritz, Klaus, Horst, Dieter, Franz, Ernst.

German, Danish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a fisherman, from Middle High German visch, German Fisch ‘fish’ + the agent suffix -er. This form of the surname is also established in some other parts of Europe, notably in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, and the Netherlands. In Poland, Czechia, Croatia, and Slovenia it is (also) established in the Slavicized forms Fiszer (Polish) and Fišer (see Fiser ). Compare Fisher .

nameMeanings.name: Carrie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of Caroline or occasionally of other girls' names beginning with the syllable Car-, now often bestowed as a name in its own right.

nameMeanings.name: Skyler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly U.S.: modern coinage, apparently derived from the Dutch surname Schuyler (meaning ‘scholar, school teacher’) and possibly adopted in honour of Philip Schuyler ( 1733–1804 ), a member of Congress and a leading figure in the American Revolution. As a given name it was earlier borne mainly by boys but is now more popular for girls.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: Carrie Skyler Fisher was born in 1505, in Buckingham, Buckinghamshire, England as the daughter of Henry Fisher and Susannah Wynne. She married Sir Thomas Ramsey in 1522, in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1576, in Buckinghamshire, England, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Carrie Skyler Fisher est née en 1505, à Buckingham, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, fille de Henry Fisher et Susannah Wynne. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Ramsey en 1522, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1576, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 71 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Carrie Skyler Fisher wurde 1505 in Buckingham, Buckinghamshire, England, als Tochter von Henry Fisher und Susannah Wynne geboren. Sie hat 1522 in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Sir Thomas Ramsey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1576 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Buckinghamshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Carrie Skyler Fisher è nata il nel 1505 nel Buckingham, Buckinghamshire, England come figlia di Henry Fisher e Susannah Wynne. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Ramsey nel 1522, nel Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1576, nel Buckinghamshire, England, all'età di 71 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Carrie Skyler Fisherは1505年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアブクインガムでHenry FisherとSusannah Wynneの娘として生まれた。1522年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアでSir Thomas Ramseyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1576年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアで71歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Carrie Skyler Fisher(은)는 1505년 영국 벅잉함실르 벅잉함에서 Henry Fisher(와)과 Susannah Wynne의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1522년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르에서 Sir Thomas Ramsey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1576년 영국 벅잉함실르에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.
